title: iris spuria var. daenensis
intro: Iris spuria var. daenensis, also known as the Danube Iris or German Iris, is a perennial plant belonging to the iris family and is native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Iris spuria var. daenensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory problems, and serve as an antiseptic agent. Herbal preparations of Iris spuria var. daenensis include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
